title: Splitting p1 (1xT75) to p2 (2xT150)
author: Andrea Argouarch
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8gnhtve

steps.instruction: At 90-100% confluency, split T75 flask into 2xT150 flasks
Turn off UV lights and clean hood with 70% ethanol

Clean items with 70% ethanol and bring into hood a. DPBS -/- DPBS, no calcium, no magnesium Thermo Fisher Catalog #14190250 b. Sterile Filtered Media DMEM, high glucose, pyruvate Thermo Fisher Catalog #11995073 Penicillin-Streptomycin Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #15140122 Fetal Bovine Serum VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #97068-091 c. Label 2xT150 flasks with ID, date, and p2, add 28 mls 28 mL of media per flask d. 0.05% Trypsin Trypsin-EDTA (0.05%), phenol red Thermo Fisher Catalog #25300062
Aspirate old media
Rinse by adding 5 mls 5 mL of DPBS per flask and gently swirling
Aspirate DPBS
Add 2 mls 2 mL of trypsin per flask and place in incubator 37 °C for 2-3 mins 00:03:00 until cells are stating to detach a. Can also gently tap the side of the flask to detach cells
Check under microscope
Add 10 mls 10 mL of fibroblast media per flask to inactivate trypsin
Collect cell suspension in a 15 ml conical
Spin at 600 rpm 600 rpm for 4 mins 00:04:00
Aspirate supernatant, being careful to not aspirate the pellet
Tap the pellet to resuspend
Add fibroblast media to 4 mls 4 mL
Add 2 ml 2 mL of cell suspension per flask
Place flask in incubator (5% CO2 , 37 o C 37 °C )
Observe and feed the next day, then feed every 2-3 days until 90-100% confluent
Throw away biohazard materials properly
Clean and sterilize hood with 70% ethanol and turn on UV
Update cell culture notes in lab notebook
